mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Implementation of Multiple Providers
mission.description:
* Added BUILDPLATFORM arg to dockerfile in order to pass arg from docker-compose build commands
* Updated make file to support a multiple provider setup
* Updated docker compose to support build + up
* updated alpha configs to actually work
* Updated sign_in template to support multiple providers
* Updated validation package to loop over the provider array and validate all providers
* Added Provider ID to Providerdata struct
* Added Provider ID to the state so callback knows which provider to us

steps.title: Review the fix
steps.description: The fix was implemented in https://github.com/oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy/pull/1418. Review the changes to understand the solution.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
## Description, Motivation and Context

This is a possible implementation of the facility discussed in #1314 to make it possible to pass through query parameters from `/oauth2/start` to the Identity Provider login URL in a secure and controlled way.  Motivating use cases include

 - wanting most requests to use `prompt=login` by default, but with the possibility to override this to `prompt=select_account` or `prompt=consent` in certain specific cases
 - passing a `login_hint` when you already know the user's email address (#1369)
 - passing additional non-standard parameters required by certain providers, e.g. `organization` when using auth0 (https://github.com/oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy/issues/1314#issuecomment-950273012)

I've introduced a generic mechanism whereby an oauth2-proxy user can configure `loginURLParameters` in the "alpha" YAML config to define the parameters that may be passed through from `/oauth2/start` to the IdP.  This is a map from the query parameter name to a set of configuration options:

```yaml
loginURLParameters:
  prompt:
    default: ["login"]
    allowed: ["select_account", "consent"]
  login_hint:
    allowAny: true
  organization:
    default: ["org1"]
```

The interaction between these options is not entirely intuitive, but the logic is intended to work as follows:

- only URL parameters named under `loginURLParameters` are candidates to be passed through from `/oauth2/start?...` to the IdP, any other parameters on the `/oauth2/start` URL will b

* Currently combining all certificates for providers if offered - I think each provider needs its own http client so we have flexibility to handle combinations of providers with different settings (i.e. 1 provider has custom cert but another does not) - this will be a big task so i will probably create another PR


## Description

* Added BUILDPLATFORM arg to dockerfile in order to pass arg from docker

I still have the following point to look at - but will open another PR for this point.

* Currently combining all certificates for providers if offered - I think each provider needs its own http client so we have flexibility to handle combinations of providers with different settings (i.e. 1 provider has custom cert but another does not) - this will be a big task so i will probably create another PR


## Description

* Added BUILDPLATFORM arg to dockerfile in order to pass arg from docker-compose build commands
* Updated make file to support a multiple provider setup
* Updated docker compose to support build + up
* updated alpha configs to actually work
* Updated sign_in template to support multiple providers
* Updated validation package to loop over the provider array and validate all providers
* Added Provider ID to Providerdata struct
* Added Provider ID to the state so callback knows which provider to use
* Added dynamic oauth2/{id}/start routes for each provider ( /oauth2/start uses [0] provider still )
* Converted providers.Provider interface to a slice in oauth struct.
* Updated unit tests to support provider struct.
* Return 404/400 if a /oauth2/{id}/start request does not match a providerid
* Added validation to check for a provider ID and providerName in each provider - will shutdown if both do not exist.
* Added Cookie refresh logic to select the correct provider
* Update documentation for multiple provider config

## Motivation and Context

<!--- Why is this change required? What problem does it solve? --> Important for environments with multiple companies etc
<!--- If it fixes an open issue, please link to the issue here. --> https://github.com/oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy/issues/926 Closes #926

## How Has This Been Tested?

<!--- Please describe in detail how you tested your changes. -->
tested locally with Keycloak and Dex as multiple providers
<!--- Include details of your testing environment, and the tests you ran to -->

* Tested locally via docker compose using keycloak and dex as providers
* Still must check new session setup with redis.
* Will test this branch version on our kubernetes feature branch sandboxes over the next month - first with 1 provider to ensure no functionality is lost, then with mutiple afterwards
* Tests still need to be updated 

Will test with Azure provider in our work environment.

<!--- see how your change affects other areas of the code, etc. -->

Currently it should not affect anything else other than breaking the sign-in-message override, as it can not support multiple providers.

- [x] I have updated the documentation/CHANGELOG accordingly.
- [x] I have created a feature (non-master) branch for my PR.

## Description, Motivation and Context

This is a possible implementation of the facility discussed in #1314 to make it possible to pass through query parameters from `/oauth2/start` to the Identity Provider login URL in a secure and controlled way.  Motivating use cases include

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
